skill.name: mailchimp-automation
skill.description: Automate Mailchimp email marketing including campaigns, audiences, subscribers, segments, and analytics via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Mailchimp email campaigns, audience management, and analytics through MCP integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 189 external_commands patterns and 1 network reference, but all are false positives. The skill is documentation for MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool calls to Mailchimp API, not actual shell commands. Network reference is the legitimate Rube MCP endpoint configuration. No malicious intent detected. Safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Streamline email marketing by automating campaign creation, audience management, and performance analytics. Integrate Mailchimp operations directly into your AI assistant workflow.
actual_capabilities: Create, configure, test, and send email campaigns programmatically
Manage audiences and subscriber lists with pagination support
Add, update, and search subscribers using MD5 hash resolution
Retrieve campaign performance reports including opens, clicks, and bounces
Schedule campaigns for future delivery or send immediately
Analyze link-level click data and subscriber engagement metrics
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Mailchimp OAuth authentication
Cannot bypass Mailchimp API rate limits for high-volume operations
Campaign sending is irreversible; test emails must be sent first
Subscriber hash computation requires MD5 of lowercase email addresses

use_cases.title: Marketing Team Campaign Automation
use_cases.description: Create and send targeted email campaigns to specific audience segments with automated test emails and approval workflows before live deployment.

use_cases.title: Subscriber List Management
use_cases.description: Bulk import new subscribers, update existing contact information, and maintain clean audience lists with status filtering and duplicate detection.

use_cases.title: Performance Analytics Dashboard
use_cases.description: Generate comprehensive campaign reports analyzing open rates, click-through rates, and subscriber engagement patterns across multiple campaigns.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Send a Simple Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create and send an email campaign to my main audience. I need to set the subject line, from name, and HTML content, then send a test email before going live.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user sending a basic newsletter

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Manage Subscriber Lists
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add 50 new subscribers to my audience list and update the status of existing subscribers who unsubscribed. Show me how to handle pagination and avoid duplicates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Regular list maintenance with bulk operations

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Campaign Analytics Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive report comparing open rates and click-through rates across all campaigns sent last month. Include link-level details for the top 3 performing campaigns.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Automated Workflow Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an automated workflow that creates a campaign from a template, segments the audience based on engagement history, sends personalized test emails, and schedules delivery for optimal send time.

output_examples.input: List all my audiences with member counts
output_examples.output: Retrieved 3 audiences: Primary Newsletter (2,450 subscribers), Product Updates (1,823 subscribers), VIP Customers (312 subscribers). Use MAILCHIMP_GET_LIST_INFO with a specific list_id to view detailed statistics including average open rate and click rate.

output_examples.input: Create a campaign for my weekly newsletter
output_examples.output: Campaign created successfully with ID: abc123def4. Status: draft. Next steps: 1) Set campaign content using MAILCHIMP_SET_CAMPAIGN_CONTENT with your HTML, 2) Send test email using MAILCHIMP_SEND_TEST_EMAIL, 3) Review and approve, 4) Send or schedule using MAILCHIMP_SEND_CAMPAIGN or MAILCHIMP_SCHEDULE_CAMPAIGN.

output_examples.input: Show me the performance of last week's campaign
output_examples.output: Campaign 'Weekly Update #42' sent on 2026-02-18: 2,450 recipients, 34.2% open rate (838 opens), 12.8% click rate (313 clicks), 0.4% bounce rate, 0.2% unsubscribe rate. Top clicked link: 'New Product Launch' with 187 clicks. Full report available via MAILCHIMP_GET_CAMPAIGN_REPORT.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing any workflow
Send test emails and obtain explicit user approval before executing MAILCHIMP_SEND_CAMPAIGN
Use pagination with appropriate count and offset values to retrieve complete datasets from large audiences
anti_patterns: Do not send campaigns without testing first; MAILCHIMP_SEND_CAMPAIGN is irreversible
Do not omit status filters when querying subscribers; always specify status=subscribed for active contacts
Do not use default pagination values for bulk operations; set count explicitly to reduce API calls

faq.question: How do I connect Mailchimp to this skill?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client configuration, then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit mailchimp. Follow the returned auth link to complete OAuth. Confirm connection status shows ACTIVE before running workflows.

faq.question: What is a subscriber_hash and how do I compute it?
faq.answer: A subscriber_hash is an MD5 hash of the lowercase email address required by many Mailchimp endpoints. Compute it using: hashlib.md5(email.lower().encode()).hexdigest() in Python or equivalent in other languages.

faq.question: Why do open rates and click rates show as decimals like 0.342?
faq.answer: Mailchimp returns rates as 0-1 fractions, not 0-100 percentages. Multiply by 100 to display as percentages. For example, 0.342 = 34.2% open rate.

faq.question: How do I avoid creating duplicate subscribers?
faq.answer: Use MAILCHIMP_ADD_OR_UPDATE_LIST_MEMBER (upsert operation) instead of MAILCHIMP_ADD_MEMBER_TO_LIST. The upsert operation updates existing subscribers and only creates new ones if they do not exist.

faq.question: Can I schedule a campaign for a specific time?
faq.answer: Yes, use MAILCHIMP_SCHEDULE_CAMPAIGN with a valid future datetime in ISO 8601 format. Past timestamps will fail. Ensure the campaign has valid content and settings before scheduling.

faq.question: What happens if I hit Mailchimp API rate limits?
faq.answer: Mailchimp enforces rate limits on API calls. For high-volume operations, use batch endpoints like MAILCHIMP_BATCH_ADD_OR_REMOVE_MEMBERS and implement exponential backoff. Pagination with larger count values reduces total API calls.
